question.text: Market participants who intend to take positions in cross-currency futures and options (contracts NOT involving Indian Rupee) are required to establish the existence of underlying exposure before entering into such contracts?

answer.text: No — they may take positions without establishing underlying exposure, subject to exchange-prescribed position limits
Yes — underlying exposure must always be established before taking any derivative position
Yes — but only for positions exceeding USD 100 million equivalent
No — there are no position limits for cross-currency contracts

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Section 8.6.2 states: "Market Participants are allowed to take positions in the cross-currency futures and exchange-traded cross-currency option contracts (contracts not involving Indian Rupee) without having to establish underlying exposure subject to the position limits as prescribed by the exchanges." **The Trap:** The USD 100 million rule applies to INR-involving currency pairs. Cross-currency (FCY-FCY) pairs have a different and more permissive regime — no underlying proof required at any level, only exchange position limits apply. **Cross-Exam Context:** INR pairs = USD 100 million no-proof threshold (above that, AD designation required). Cross-currency pairs = no underlying proof needed, just adhere to exchange position limits. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Two regimes: INR pairs (USD 100M threshold) vs. cross-currency pairs (no underlying proof, just position limits). Cross-currency is simpler and more permissive.
